description: Full redesign of chandlerhardy.com — keeping Next.js 15 + Tailwind but replacing the entire design with a distinctive, minimal dark-mode-first aesthetic that highlights real engineering impact (51 MRs, 4k users, 40% US cattle market). Monospace typography accents for code-adjacent content. Not generic AI-gradient. Sections: Hero, About, Experience (Performance Beef), Projects (4 real projects), Skills, Contact.
skillHints: Use Playwright MCP to visually verify each UI section after implementing it

userStories.title: Set up design system foundations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a coherent design token system so that every component uses consistent colors, typography, and spacing.
acceptanceCriteria: tailwind.config.ts updated with custom color palette: primary (#E8E8E8 light / #1A1A1A dark), accent (#4ADE80 green), muted (#6B7280), surface (#111111 dark / #FAFAFA light)
Custom font config added: 'JetBrains Mono' (monospace) for code/accent text, 'Inter' for body — both via next/font/google in layout.tsx
globals.css updated with CSS custom properties mapping the new palette for both light and dark themes
Existing FloatingParticles, FloatingShapes, ScrollProgress, ThemeTransition, MagneticHover components removed from page.tsx (design is clean, not effects-heavy)
ThemeProvider kept, defaultTheme changed to 'dark'
npm run build passes with no errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Design system foundations implemented. Fixed NODE_ENV=production issue in build script, migrated ThemeProvider to next-themes in layout.tsx, added JetBrains Mono + Inter via next/font/google, updated tailwind.config.js with new color palette, updated globals.css with CSS custom properties for light/dark themes, removed FloatingParticles/FloatingShapes/ScrollProgress/ThemeTransition from page.tsx, added 'use client' directives to components missing them.

userStories.title: Redesign Header component
userStories.description: As a site visitor, I want a minimal, clean header so that navigation feels intentional and professional.
acceptanceCriteria: Header is a sticky top bar with subtle border-bottom (1px, muted color)
Left side: 'CH' monogram in JetBrains Mono font, slightly larger
Right side: nav links — Work, About, Contact — in Inter, small, uppercase letter-spacing
Mobile: hamburger menu collapses nav to vertical drawer
No gradient backgrounds, no blur glass effect — clean solid background with slight opacity
Dark/light toggle kept but moved to right of nav
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP
userStories.notes: Header redesigned: sticky top bar with 1px border-bottom, 'CH' monogram in JetBrains Mono font-mono on the left, nav links (Work, About, Contact) in Inter small uppercase with tracking-widest on the right, ThemeToggle to the right of nav. No backdrop-blur, no gradient — clean solid bg-background/95. Mobile hamburger collapses to vertical drawer. Build and typecheck pass.

userStories.title: Redesign Hero section
userStories.description: As a hiring manager, I want to immediately understand who Chandler is and what makes him compelling so that I decide to keep reading.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section is full viewport height with content vertically centered
Top label: small monospace text '// full-stack engineer' in accent green, above the name
Name 'Chandler Hardy' in large (text-5xl md:text-7xl) Inter bold, white
Tagline below name: 'Building data platforms at national scale. 51 merged MRs. 4,000+ users. Self-taught.' — plain text, muted color, text-xl
Two CTAs: primary 'View My Work' (links to #projects, accent green border button) and secondary 'Get In Touch' (links to #contact, ghost button)
No hero image, no avatar, no blob gradient — typography-only hero
Subtle animated cursor blink after the tagline text (CSS only, no JS library)
Mobile: text scales down, buttons stack vertically
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP
userStories.notes: Hero redesigned: full-viewport-height section with vertically centered content, '// full-stack engineer' monospace label in #4ADE80, 'Chandler Hardy' in text-5xl md:text-7xl Inter bold, tagline in text-xl muted-foreground with CSS cursor blink animation, two CTAs (View My Work -> #projects green border, Get In Touch -> #contact ghost), no image/avatar/gradient, buttons stack vertically on mobile.

userStories.title: Redesign About section
userStories.description: As a hiring manager, I want to understand Chandler's background and what drives him so that I can evaluate cultural fit.
acceptanceCriteria: Section ID 'about', two-column layout on desktop (prose left, stats right), single column mobile
Left column: 2-3 short paragraphs in prose style — self-taught origin, current work at Performance Beef, what he's building on the side
Right column: 3-4 stat cards with large numbers — '51 MRs merged', '4,000+ users', '2 YOE', '40% US cattle market' — each with a small label below
Stat numbers in JetBrains Mono, large (text-4xl), accent green color
Section heading: '// about' in monospace accent style above main heading
No bullet lists, no skill tags in this section
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP
userStories.notes: About section redesigned: section ID 'about', two-column layout (prose left, stat cards right) on desktop, single column mobile. Left: 3 prose paragraphs covering self-taught origin, Performance Livestock Analytics work, and side projects. Right: 4 stat cards with text-4xl font-mono #4ADE80 numbers ('51', '4,000+', '2 YOE', '40%') and small muted labels. Section heading '// about' in monospace accent green above 'Who I Am'. No bullet lists, no skill tags. Build and typecheck pass.

userStories.title: Add Experience section (Performance Beef)
userStories.description: As a hiring manager, I want to see Chandler's professional experience highlighted so that I understand the scope and impact of his work.
acceptanceCriteria: New Experience section component created at components/Experience.tsx
Section added to page.tsx between About and Projects
Section ID 'experience', heading pattern '// experience' + 'Where I've Worked'
Single experience card for Performance Livestock Analytics: role 'Software Engineer', dates 'Feb 2024 – Present'
Description paragraph about the platform (data platform, 4k producers, 40% US cattle market)
4-5 bullet points covering key contributions: yard sheet dashboard, nav bar customization, mass animal ID system, protocol management refactor, kill sheet tracking
Tech stack tags at bottom of card: PHP, jQuery, Go, MongoDB, Highcharts
Card has subtle left border accent in green
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP

userStories.title: Redesign Projects section
userStories.description: As a hiring manager, I want to see Chandler's personal projects with clear tech stacks and context so that I can evaluate his independent engineering skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Section ID 'projects', heading pattern '// work' + 'Selected Projects'
Grid layout: 2 columns on desktop, 1 column on mobile
All 4 projects shown (Crooked Finger, Elucidate Chess, GreenLine, Ralph) — remove any slice(0,3) limitation
Each project card: title, one-line description, tech stack tags (from project.tags), status badge, links (GitHub icon always, Live link if liveUrl exists)
Cards have dark surface background (#111 dark / #F5F5F5 light), subtle border, hover lifts card with shadow
No hero images on cards (image files don't exist) — cards are text/tag based
Status badges: Crooked Finger='Live' (green), Elucidate Chess='In Progress' (yellow), GreenLine='Complete' (blue), Ralph='Tool' (gray)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP

userStories.title: Redesign Skills section
userStories.description: As a hiring manager, I want to see Chandler's skills organized by category so that I can quickly assess fit for my role.
acceptanceCriteria: Section ID 'skills', heading pattern '// stack' + 'Technical Skills'
Skills organized in a grid of category groups, not a flat tag cloud

Categories: Languages (TypeScript, Python, Go, PHP, Swift, JavaScript), Frontend (React, Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS, Vue.js), Backend (FastAPI, Node.js, GraphQL, REST APIs), Database (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, MySQL), AI/ML (Claude API, OpenAI, Gemini, OpenRouter, MCP, RAG), Infrastructure (Docker, OCI, Vercel, nginx, n8n, CI/CD)
Each category has a monospace label and the skills listed as small pill tags below
Old Skills.tsx component replaced with new implementation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP

userStories.title: Redesign Contact section and Footer
userStories.description: As a hiring manager, I want a clear way to reach Chandler so that I can start a conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact section ID 'contact', heading '// contact' + 'Get In Touch'
Simple layout: short paragraph ('Open to remote full-stack roles. Reach out directly.'), then email address as a large clickable link (hardych04@gmail.com), then row of icon links: GitHub, LinkedIn
No contact form — direct email link only
Footer: single row, left side '© 2025 Chandler Hardy', right side social links (GitHub, LinkedIn), minimal border-top
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP

userStories.title: Final integration, cleanup, and build verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the entire redesigned site to build cleanly and be free of unused code so that it is production-ready for PR review.
acceptanceCriteria: All unused component files removed: FloatingParticles.tsx, FloatingShapes.tsx, ScrollProgress.tsx, ThemeTransition.tsx, MagneticHover.tsx, TypewriterText.tsx, EnhancedButton.tsx (if not used by any active component)
No TypeScript errors across all components
npm run build completes successfully with no errors
page.tsx only imports components that are actually used
Dark mode is the default theme (ThemeProvider defaultTheme='dark')
Site is fully mobile-responsive — no horizontal scroll on 375px viewport
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP
